operating_state: Total revenues increased 5.1% to $681.0 billion driven by 5.0% net sales growth, with comparable sales growth of 4.8% in the U.S. from transaction and unit volume increases across grocery and health categories. U.S. segment delivered 4.8% comparable sales growth with eCommerce contributing 2.9%, while Sam's Club achieved 4.7% growth with eCommerce contributing 2.3%. International segment grew 6.3% despite $3.2 billion negative currency impact, with positive comparable sales across all markets.
cost_structure: Gross profit rate improved 40 basis points to 24.1% driven by competitive price alignment and higher margin business growth, partially offset by merchandise mix shifts. Operating expenses increased 36 basis points to 20.7% due to higher variable pay from exceeding performance targets, increased marketing expenses, and higher depreciation costs. Operating income margin expanded 15 basis points to 4.4% with operating income growing to $29.3 billion.
material_events: Completed VIZIO acquisition for $1.9 billion and divested JD.com investment generating $3.6 billion proceeds. Approved fiscal 2026 dividend increase to $0.94 per share from $0.83 and repurchased $4.5 billion shares with $12.0 billion remaining under authorization. Reached derivative litigation settlement providing $123 million insurance proceeds with five-year governance maintenance requirements. International CEO and CEO established new 10b5-1 trading plans through 2026.
macro_exposures: Continued uncertainty from inflationary trends, tariffs, trade restrictions, global currency fluctuations, and supply chain pressures impacting operations. Currency fluctuations negatively impacted International segment by $3.2 billion. Pricing strategies include absorbing cost increases, selective price reductions, and supplier cost reduction initiatives.
forward_outlook: Projected capital expenditures of $21-25 billion for fiscal 2026 focused on technology, supply chain and customer initiatives. Strategic allocation prioritizes automation, eCommerce, supply chain and store investments. Management committed to growth through omni-channel experience, margin improvement through productivity and business mix optimization, and returns enhancement through disciplined capital spending.
uncertainty_profile: Free cash flow decreased to $12.7 billion from $15.1 billion due to $3.2 billion capital expenditure increase. Working capital deficit increased to $17.1 billion driven by higher borrowings and payable timing. Outstanding purchase obligations total $37.2 billion. Effective tax rate of 23.4% may fluctuate based on investment valuations, operations mix, and regulatory developments.
